role.name: QA Tester
role.description: Find edge cases before real users find them in production.

chapters.title: It Works on My Machine
chapters.story: Developer proudly says, “Mere system pe chal raha hai.” QA enters the room like a horror movie background score.
chapters.task: Test the payment form before release.

outcome.title: Happy Path Trap
outcome.description: Live user taps Pay 10 times. System creates 10 invoices, 10 SMS alerts, and one finance team meltdown.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

choices.text: Test empty data, invalid card, duplicate click, slow network

outcome.title: Bug Hunter Ending
outcome.description: You catch duplicate payment risk before release. Developer cries softly but respects you.
outcome.moralLesson: QA protects the product from real users.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: QA protects the product from real users.

outcome.title: Screenshot Certified Disaster
outcome.description: The screenshot was from localhost. Production behaves like it drank five energy drinks.
outcome.moralLesson: Evidence must be tested, not blindly trusted.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Evidence must be tested, not blindly trusted.

miniGame.title: Choose the Real Payment Test Cases
miniGame.instructions: Select every test case that must be checked before release.
miniGame.prompt: The payment form is going live. Which cases are necessary for responsible QA?
miniGame.hint: Think happy path, validation, failure, and boundary cases.

options.text: Successful payment with valid card details
Test only with your own name because it feels lucky
options.helperText: Lucky is not a QA strategy.

miniGame.successMessage: Bug hunter mode activated. The payment form is now scared to misbehave.
miniGame.failureMessage: QA missed a case. The payment form now accepts payment only when Mercury is in retrograde.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice bug reporting workflow for a QA Tester situation involving edge cases and real-user behavior.
safetyGuardrails: Do not hide critical bugs to meet deadlines.
Protect test data and user privacy.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the bug reporting workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: QA simulation: report evidence honestly and do not hide critical defects to meet timelines. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: QA Lead says: At beginner level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the bug reporting workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not hide critical bugs to meet deadlines.
Protect test data and user privacy.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: OTP Loop Mystery
chapters.story: Login OTP works once, then fails after app restart. Developer says, “Maybe user typed emotion instead of OTP.”

choices.text: Test only one successful OTP login

outcome.title: One-Time Tester Ending
outcome.description: Release goes live. Users restart app and enter OTP more times than a TV serial flashback.
outcome.moralLesson: Login testing needs session, retry, expiry, and restart cases.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Login testing needs session, retry, expiry, and restart cases.

choices.text: Test wrong OTP, resend, expiry, app restart, and session timeout

outcome.title: Auth Guardian Ending
outcome.description: You catch the broken token refresh flow before users start uninstalling with passion.
outcome.moralLesson: Authentication quality depends on negative and lifecycle testing.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Authentication quality depends on negative and lifecycle testing.

choices.text: Tell users to not close the app

outcome.title: Never Sleep App Ending
outcome.description: Users are advised to keep the app open forever. Battery percentage files a complaint.
outcome.moralLesson: QA should verify real behavior, not ask users to behave perfectly.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: QA should verify real behavior, not ask users to behave perfectly.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice regression planning workflow for a QA Tester situation involving authentication edge cases.
safetyGuardrails: Do not hide critical bugs to meet deadlines.
Protect test data and user privacy.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the regression planning workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: QA simulation: report evidence honestly and do not hide critical defects to meet timelines. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: QA Lead says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the regression planning workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not hide critical bugs to meet deadlines.
Protect test data and user privacy.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: Release Notes Ghost
chapters.story: A build arrives with release notes: “Minor fixes.” The APK size increased by 30 MB and three new screens are hiding inside.

choices.text: Trust release notes and test only one bug

outcome.title: Ghost Bug Ending
outcome.description: A hidden screen breaks checkout. Everyone discovers it during the demo, because demos love drama.
outcome.moralLesson: Regression risk must be checked when scope is unclear.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Regression risk must be checked when scope is unclear.

choices.text: Ask for change list, compare build, and run targeted regression

outcome.title: Regression Shield Ending
outcome.description: You identify hidden changes, test impacted flows, and save the demo from becoming a meme.
outcome.moralLesson: Clear change information improves testing quality.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Clear change information improves testing quality.

choices.text: Reject build because release notes are boring

outcome.title: Drama Review Ending
outcome.description: You are partly right but communicate badly. The team starts naming builds after your mood.
outcome.moralLesson: Good QA escalates with evidence, not irritation.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Good QA escalates with evidence, not irritation.
